# DESIGN.md Format Specification

This document defines the exact structure, section requirements, and formatting rules for DESIGN.md files generated by `dmdg`.

## File Header

Every DESIGN.md begins with exactly three HTML comment lines, followed by a blank line:

```
<!-- Generated: {YYYY-MM-DD} | Source: {url} | Pages: {count} | Framework: {name|none} -->
<!-- This is not the official design system. Colors, fonts, and spacing may not be 100% accurate. -->
<!-- Generated by design-md-generator -->
```

- `{YYYY-MM-DD}` -- ISO 8601 date of generation.
- `{url}` -- the root URL that was crawled (e.g. `https://vercel.com`).
- `{count}` -- total pages analyzed.
- `{name|none}` -- detected CSS framework (`tailwind`, `bootstrap`, `none`, etc.).

## Title

```
# Design System: {SiteName}
```

`{SiteName}` is the proper-cased brand name (e.g. `Vercel`, `Stripe`, `Linear`). One blank line before section 1.

---

## Sections Overview

A complete DESIGN.md contains **9 numbered sections** plus one optional half-section (6.5). Sections are always in this order. No section may be omitted except 6.5.

| # | Title | Required |
|---|-------|----------|
| 1 | Visual Theme & Atmosphere | yes |
| 2 | Color Palette & Roles | yes |
| 3 | Typography Rules | yes |
| 4 | Component Stylings | yes |
| 5 | Layout Principles | yes |
| 6 | Depth & Elevation | yes |
| 6.5 | Motion & Transitions | no |
| 7 | Do's and Don'ts | yes |
| 8 | Responsive Behavior | yes |
| 9 | Agent Prompt Guide | yes |

---

## Section 1 -- Visual Theme & Atmosphere

**Title format:**

```
## 1. Visual Theme & Atmosphere
```

**Required content:**

1. **Opening paragraph** (100--180 words). An atmospheric, opinionated description of the site's visual personality. Must reference at least two specific CSS values (hex colors, font names, spacing values) inline with backtick formatting. Avoid generic praise; describe what makes the design distinct.

2. **Second/third paragraph** (50--100 words). Technical elaboration on the most distinctive design technique (e.g. shadow-as-border, light-weight headlines, chromatic shadows). Include concrete CSS values.

3. **Key Characteristics list**. A bullet list of 6--10 items. Each item follows this format:

```
- Descriptive phrase — technical detail with `css-value`
```

Example:

```
- Geist Sans with extreme negative letter-spacing (-2.4px at display) — text as compressed infrastructure
- Shadow-as-border technique: `box-shadow: 0px 0px 0px 1px` replaces traditional borders throughout
```

**Word count guidance:** 150--250 words total (excluding the Key Characteristics list).

**Optional subsections:** None.

---

## Section 2 -- Color Palette & Roles

**Title format:**

```
## 2. Color Palette & Roles
```

**Required subsections** -- organize colors into semantic groups using `###` headings. Use whichever groups apply to the site. Common groups:

- `### Primary`
- `### Accent Colors`
- `### Workflow Colors` (if the brand uses workflow-specific colors)
- `### Interactive`
- `### Neutral Scale`
- `### Surface & Borders`
- `### Shadows & Depth` or `### Shadow Colors`

**Minimum:** 8 colors across all groups.

**Color entry format:**

```
- **Color Name** (`#hexval`): Role description. Personality or usage note.
```

Rules for color entries:

- Hex values are always 6-digit lowercase (e.g. `#533afd`, never `#533AFD` or `#53a`).
- CSS custom property names go in backticks after the hex if known: `` `--hds-color-heading-solid` ``.
- HSLA/RGBA values are acceptable for shadows and semi-transparent colors: `rgba(50,50,93,0.25)`.
- Each entry must describe both the functional role and the visual/brand character.

**Optional subsection:**

```
### Dark Mode Overrides
```

Include only when the site has a distinct dark theme with different token values.

**Example:**

```
### Primary
- **Stripe Purple** (`#533afd`): Primary brand color, CTA backgrounds, link text. A saturated blue-violet that anchors the system.
- **Deep Navy** (`#061b31`): `--hds-color-heading-solid`. Primary heading color. Not black -- a very dark blue that adds warmth.
```

---

## Section 3 -- Typography Rules

**Title format:**

```
## 3. Typography Rules
```

**Required subsections:**

### `### Font Family`

List primary and monospace font families with their full fallback stacks. Note any OpenType features enabled globally.

```
- **Primary**: `Geist`, with fallbacks: `Arial, Apple Color Emoji, Segoe UI Emoji`
- **Monospace**: `Geist Mono`, with fallbacks: `ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo`
- **OpenType Features**: `"liga"` enabled globally; `"tnum"` for tabular numbers on captions.
```

### `### Hierarchy`

A markdown table with these exact columns:

| Role | Font | Size | Weight | Line Height | Letter Spacing | Notes |
|------|------|------|--------|-------------|----------------|-------|

An optional `Features` column may be added between `Letter Spacing` and `Notes` when the site uses OpenType feature sets.

Column rules:

- **Role**: Descriptive name in Title Case (e.g. `Display Hero`, `Body Large`, `Caption Small`).
- **Font**: Font family name only, no fallbacks (e.g. `sohne-var`).
- **Size**: `{px}px ({rem}rem)` -- e.g. `48px (3.00rem)`. Use 2 decimal places for rem.
- **Weight**: Numeric only. Never use `bold`, `semibold`, `light`. Use `300`, `400`, `500`, `600`, `700`. Ranges like `300-400` are acceptable.
- **Line Height**: Unitless ratio (e.g. `1.50`). Add a descriptor in parentheses for extremes: `1.00 (tight)`, `1.80 (relaxed)`.
- **Letter Spacing**: Value in px (e.g. `-2.4px`, `-0.96px`) or `normal`.
- **Features**: OpenType feature tags in backticks (e.g. `ss01`, `tnum`).
- **Notes**: Brief usage note. Mention `text-transform: uppercase` here when applicable.

**Row count:** 12--20 rows.

### `### Principles`

3--5 bullet points explaining the typographic philosophy. Each principle has a **bold label** followed by a colon and explanation. Reference specific values.

```
- **Compression as identity**: Geist Sans at display sizes uses -2.4px letter-spacing -- the most aggressive negative tracking of any major design system.
```

---

## Section 4 -- Component Stylings

**Title format:**

```
## 4. Component Stylings
```

**Required subsections** (use `###` headings). Include whichever apply:

- `### Buttons`
- `### Cards & Containers`
- `### Badges / Tags / Pills` or `### Distinctive Components`
- `### Inputs & Forms`
- `### Navigation`
- `### Image Treatment`

Each component variant gets a `**Bold Variant Name**` heading followed by a property list. Properties use this format:

```
**Primary Dark**
- Background: `#171717`
- Text: `#ffffff`
- Padding: 8px 16px (vertical horizontal)
- Radius: 6px
- Font: 16px sohne-var weight 400, `"ss01"`
- Hover: background shifts to `#4434d4`
- Focus: `2px solid var(--ds-focus-color)` outline
- Use: Primary CTA ("Start Deploying", "Get Started")
```

Rules:

- All CSS values in backticks.
- Include states: Hover, Focus, Active, Disabled -- whichever were observed.
- The `Use:` line describes where the component appears on the site.
- Group related variants under the same `###` subsection.

---

## Section 5 -- Layout Principles

**Title format:**

```
## 5. Layout Principles
```

**Required subsections:**

### `### Spacing System`

- Base unit (e.g. `8px`).
- The full spacing scale as a comma-separated list.

### `### Grid & Container`

- Max content width (e.g. `1200px`).
- Hero layout pattern.
- Feature section column counts.
- Notable full-width or contained patterns.

### `### Whitespace Philosophy`

2--3 bold-labeled bullet points describing the site's spatial character. Each must reference specific pixel values.

### `### Border Radius Scale`

A list of radius tokens from smallest to largest:

```
- Micro (2px): Inline code snippets
- Standard (6px): Buttons, functional elements
- Full Pill (9999px): Badges, status pills
```

---

## Section 6 -- Depth & Elevation

**Title format:**

```
## 6. Depth & Elevation
```

**Required content:**

1. **Shadow scale table** with these columns:

| Level | Treatment | Use |
|-------|-----------|-----|

Each row represents one elevation level from flat (Level 0) to the highest elevation. The `Treatment` column contains the full CSS `box-shadow` value in backticks (or "No shadow" for Level 0). Include 4--6 levels.

2. **Shadow Philosophy paragraph** (50--100 words). Describe the system's approach to depth -- whether shadows are chromatic, neutral, layered, minimal, etc. Reference specific RGBA values.

**Optional subsection:**

### `### Decorative Depth`

Gradients, section borders, background color shifts, or other non-shadow depth techniques.

---

## Section 6.5 -- Motion & Transitions (Optional)

**Title format:**

```
## 6.5. Motion & Transitions
```

**Include this section only when** the crawled site has observable motion data (CSS transitions, keyframe animations, or animation library usage).

**Subsections when present:**

- `### Duration Scale` -- table of timing tokens (e.g. `fast: 150ms`, `normal: 300ms`).
- `### Timing Functions` -- easing curves with CSS values.
- `### Keyframe Animations` -- named animations with description.

**If no motion data exists, omit the entire section.** Do not generate placeholder content.

---

## Section 7 -- Do's and Don'ts

**Title format:**

```
## 7. Do's and Don'ts
```

**Required subsections:**

### `### Do`

8--12 bullet items. Each item format:

```
- Action to take -- why it matters, with specific `css-value` or token reference
```

Example:

```
- Use shadow-as-border (`0px 0px 0px 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`) instead of CSS borders -- maintains the shadow-layer architecture
- Use `#171717` instead of `#000000` for primary text -- the micro-warmth prevents harshness
```

### `### Don't`

8--12 bullet items. Same format as Do items but describing what to avoid:

```
- Don't use weight 700 on body text -- 600 is the maximum for headings only
- Don't use pill radius (9999px) on primary buttons -- pills are reserved for badges and tags
```

Every item must contain:

1. The specific action to take or avoid.
2. The reason why.
3. At least one concrete CSS value, token name, or threshold.

---

## Section 8 -- Responsive Behavior

**Title format:**

```
## 8. Responsive Behavior
```

**Required subsections:**

### `### Breakpoints`

A markdown table:

| Name | Width | Key Changes |
|------|-------|-------------|

4--7 rows covering mobile through large desktop. Width column uses ranges (e.g. `640-1024px`) or inequalities (e.g. `<640px`, `>1400px`).

### `### Touch Targets`

3--5 bullet points on button padding, link spacing, and tap-target sizing for mobile.

### `### Collapsing Strategy`

6--10 bullet points describing how specific components adapt across breakpoints. Format:

```
- Component: desktop behavior -> mobile behavior
```

Use `->` (arrow) to show the transformation.

### `### Image Behavior`

3--5 bullet points on responsive image handling, border treatment at small sizes, and aspect ratio preservation.

---

## Section 9 -- Agent Prompt Guide

**Title format:**

```
## 9. Agent Prompt Guide
```

This section enables AI agents to reproduce the design system without reading the full document.

**Required subsections:**

### `### Quick Color Reference`

A flat bullet list of 8--12 colors with their roles and hex values. No grouping, no personality notes -- just fast lookup:

```
- Primary CTA: Stripe Purple (`#533afd`)
- Background: Pure White (`#ffffff`)
- Heading text: Deep Navy (`#061b31`)
- Body text: Slate (`#64748d`)
```

### `### Example Component Prompts`

5--6 self-contained prompt strings, each 100--200 words. Each prompt must be a complete instruction that an AI agent can execute without referencing any other section. Include:

- Element type (hero, card, button, navigation, badge, dark section).
- Full CSS values: font family, size, weight, line-height, letter-spacing, color, background, padding, radius, shadow, border, and OpenType features.
- State variations where relevant (hover color, focus ring).

Format each prompt as a quoted string inside a bullet:

```
- "Create a hero section on white background. Headline at 48px Geist weight 600, line-height 1.00, letter-spacing -2.4px, color #171717. Subtitle at 20px weight 400, line-height 1.80, color #4d4d4d. Dark CTA button (#171717, 6px radius, 8px 16px padding) and ghost button (white, shadow-border rgba(0,0,0,0.08) 0px 0px 0px 1px, 6px radius)."
```

### `### Iteration Guide`

6--8 numbered steps. Each step is a concise directive with at least one concrete value:

```
1. Always use shadow-as-border instead of CSS border -- `0px 0px 0px 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` is the foundation
2. Letter-spacing scales with font size: -2.4px at 48px, -1.28px at 32px, normal at 14px
```

---

## Global Formatting Rules

These rules apply across all sections:

### Values

- **Hex colors**: Always 6-digit lowercase (`#533afd`, never `#533AFD`, `#53a`, or `#533AED`).
- **Font weights**: Always numeric (`300`, `400`, `500`, `600`, `700`). Never `light`, `regular`, `medium`, `semibold`, `bold`.
- **Sizes**: Primary unit is `px` with rem equivalent in parentheses: `48px (3.00rem)`. Use 2 decimal places for rem.
- **CSS values**: Always wrapped in backticks: `rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`, `#171717`, `"ss01"`, `box-shadow`, `border-radius`.
- **Font names**: Always wrapped in backticks: `Geist`, `sohne-var`, `SourceCodePro`.
- **CSS property names**: Always wrapped in backticks: `font-feature-settings`, `letter-spacing`, `box-shadow`.

### Text

- **Em-dash** (--) separates description from technical detail in bullet items. Use `--` (double hyphen) in the markdown source; renderers convert to em-dash.
- **No consecutive blank lines.** Maximum one blank line between any two elements.
- **Section headings** (`##`) are preceded by exactly one blank line.
- **Subsection headings** (`###`) are preceded by exactly one blank line.

### Structure

- Sections are numbered with a period: `## 1.`, `## 2.`, through `## 9.`
- Section 6.5 uses `## 6.5.` as its number.
- No table of contents. The section headings serve as navigation.
- No front-matter or YAML metadata beyond the three HTML comment lines.
- The file ends with a newline after the last line of content.

### Tables

- All tables use standard markdown pipe syntax.
- Header row is followed by a separator row with dashes and pipes.
- Cell content does not use leading/trailing whitespace padding for alignment (let the renderer handle it).
- Empty cells contain a single dash (`-`) or are left blank.

### Word Counts

| Section | Target Range |
|---------|-------------|
| 1. Visual Theme & Atmosphere | 150--250 words (prose only) |
| 2. Color Palette & Roles | 8+ color entries, no word limit |
| 3. Typography Rules | 12--20 table rows + 3--5 principles |
| 4. Component Stylings | 3+ component groups, no word limit |
| 5. Layout Principles | 4 subsections, no word limit |
| 6. Depth & Elevation | 4--6 table rows + 50--100 word philosophy |
| 6.5. Motion & Transitions | Optional, no word limit |
| 7. Do's and Don'ts | 8--12 items per subsection |
| 8. Responsive Behavior | 4 subsections, no word limit |
| 9. Agent Prompt Guide | 8--12 colors, 5--6 prompts, 6--8 steps |
